questions.question: What structural problem makes ASR harder than per-clip classification?
options: Audio files are larger than images
Audio frames don't align one-to-one with characters — "okay" might take 200ms or 1200ms, silence punctuates, phoneme durations vary, and the number of output tokens isn't known in advance
Microphones add too much noise
Text has no numeric representation
questions.explanation: The three formulations — CTC, RNN-T, and attention encoder-decoder — are three different answers to this alignment problem.

questions.question: How does CTC handle alignment, and what is its known weakness?
options: It forces one character per frame

Per-frame distributions over V+1 tokens including a blank; any alignment that collapses (repeats merged, blanks removed) to the target counts toward the loss — but the conditional independence assumption means no internal language model
It uses dynamic programming over words only
It requires hand-labeled frame alignments
questions.explanation: Non-autoregressive, streamable, zero lookahead — used by wav2vec 2.0 and MMS. The missing LM is fixed externally via beam search or shallow fusion.

questions.question: What does RNN-T add over CTC, and at what cost?
options: Nothing — it's CTC with a new name

A predictor network embedding token history plus a joiner combining it with the encoder frame — an internal LM while staying streamable; the cost is complex, memory-hungry training (a 3D loss lattice with specialized kernels)
Bidirectional attention over the future
A diffusion decoder for text
questions.explanation: RNN-T explicitly models the conditional dependence CTC ignored, which is why Google's on-device ASR and NVIDIA Parakeet use it for streaming.

questions.question: Why isn't attention encoder-decoder (Whisper-style) streamable by default?
options: It runs only on TPUs

The decoder cross-attends ANYWHERE in the audio with no alignment constraint and generates autoregressively — highest offline quality, but latency scales with output length and streaming requires restricting attention (e.g., chunked Whisper-Streaming)
It outputs all tokens simultaneously
Its encoder cannot process partial audio
questions.explanation: The 2026 SOTA WERs are nearly tied (Parakeet-TDT 1.40% vs Whisper-Large-v3-turbo 1.58% on test-clean) — the formulation choice is really a deployment choice.

questions.question: How is Word Error Rate computed, and what are the usability thresholds?
options: Percentage of perfectly transcribed clips
(S + D + I) / N — substitutions, deletions, insertions over reference word count, i.e., word-level Levenshtein; above 20% is generally unusable, below 5% is human parity for read speech
BLEU score against the reference
Character accuracy divided by duration
questions.explanation: Note N is the REFERENCE length, so WER can exceed 100% with enough insertions. Pure CTC systems sit around 1.8-2.1% on test-clean vs ~1.4-1.6% for RNN-T/attention SOTA.
